video:video.video:title: Correspondent's dinner shooting suspect sent writings to family minutes before attack
video:video.video:description: The writings, sent shortly before shots were fired Saturday night at the Washington Hilton, made repeated references to President Donald Trump without naming him directly and alluded to grievances over a range of administration actions, including U.S. strikes on boats accused of smuggling drugs in t

video:video.video:title: A sheep gives birth to rare sextuplets on a Vermont farm
video:video.video:description: A sheep at Clover & Bee Farm in Underhill gave birth to a rare batch of six lambs earlier this month. (AP video: Amanda Swinhart)

video:video.video:title: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe breaks the fabled 2-hour marathon barrier by 30 seconds in London
video:video.video:description: A pair of African distance runners took down what was once among the most unthinkable records in sports on Sunday, shattering the long-unapproachable two-hour barrier in the 26.2-mile (42.2-kilometer) marathon.

video:video.video:title: Iran's foreign minister visits Russia following his meetings in Pakistan
video:video.video:description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi landed in St. Petersburg on Monday morning ahead of a meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin.

video:video.video:title: Tornadoes in northern Texas leave at least 2 dead and destroy multiple homes
video:video.video:description: A tornado-producing thunderstorm left at least two people dead in northern Texas and displaced at least 20 families, with many homes sustaining major damage, authorities said Sunday.

video:video.video:title: How this surgeon keeps a Sudan hospital functioning on the war's front line
video:video.video:description: As Sudan's war raged around him, Dr. Jamal Eltaeb was certain only about one thing: keep working. The hospital was bombed several times, medical supplies ran out and most of the staff had fled, leaving him in charge.

video:video.video:title: How tech can tell if a soybean comes from an illegally deforested area
video:video.video:description: Soybean farming is one of the biggest causes of tropical deforestation in the world. Now scientists have developed a technique that can accurately predict where a bean came from, helping to identify crops harvested in illegally logged areas.

video:video.video:title: Israeli army releases video it says shows the destruction of Hezbollah infrastructure in Lebanon
video:video.video:description: The Israeli army released a video on Monday showing what it claimed was the destruction of infrastructure belonging to Hezbollah in southern Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Two former Israeli prime ministers join forces against Netanyahu in upcoming elections
video:video.video:description: Two Israeli political heavyweights on Sunday said they would join forces in elections scheduled for later this year in an effort to unseat longtime Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

video:video.video:title: Record snowfall hits Moscow
video:video.video:description: A heavy snowfall hit Moscow overnight on Monday, leading to 200 snow-laden trees falling, damaging cars and power line and causing delays in trains and flights.

video:video.video:title: Emergency services respond after Russia strikes civilian and port infrastructure in Odesa
video:video.video:description: Eleven people, including two children, sought medical treatment following the strikes in Odesa, said Marina Averina, the spokesperson for the State Emergency Service of Ukraine in Odesa region.

video:video.video:title: Safety concerns discussed as AI becomes a dominant trend at Beijing car show
video:video.video:description: Artificial intelligence was a major theme at this year's Auto China car show on Saturday. Amid the hundreds of vehicles on display, many products were harnessing the power of AI to improve the driving experience.

video:video.video:title: Virginia Supreme Court considers whether to block voter-approved US House map favoring Democrats
video:video.video:description: Officials react as Virginia Supreme Court weighs whether to block new U.S. House districts approved by voters. Republicans are challenging the map that favors Democrats.

video:video.video:title: Kentucky Derby favorite Renegade gets a sponge bath after workout
video:video.video:description: After a morning workout at Churchill Downs, Kentucky Derby favorite Renegade is given a spongebath. Renegade trainer Todd Pletcher says he is the best horse in the Derby.

video:video.video:title: 'Widow's Bay' weird vibes location house
video:video.video:description: Matthew Rhys and Kate O'Flynn, who star in upcoming horror comedy series "Widow's Bay," recall one New England location house that had "its own energy."

video:video.video:title: Elderly woman in Ukraine taken to safety by unmanned vehicle
video:video.video:description: A woman was walking on crutches in a dangerous section in Ukraine’s Lymanske region before taken to safety by Ukrainian drone operators.

video:video.video:title: Merz says the American nation “is being humiliated” by the Iranian leadership
video:video.video:description: The German Chancellor said on Monday that U.S. ‘is being humiliated’ by the Iranian leadership, and criticized America for going into the Iran war without any strategy, saying this also makes it harder to end the conflict.

video:video.video:title: Leavitt blames Democrats for promoting rhetoric that fueled 'cult of hatred' against Trump
video:video.video:description: Karoline Leavitt, who was sitting at the head table near Trump on Saturday, said that those who call Trump a fascist or compare him to Adolf Hitler are “fueling this kind of violence."

video:video.video:title: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive in US for four-day state visit
video:video.video:description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrived in the U.S. Monday for a four-day state visit marking America’s 250th anniversary.

video:video.video:title: Faith leaders in Alabama urge end to Confederate holidays rooted in division
video:video.video:description: People gathered outside the Montgomery home that served as the First White House of the Confederacy to urge state leaders to halt the state's observance of three Confederate holidays.

video:video.video:title: On World Penguin Day: how scientists track them on their 12,000 km migration journey
video:video.video:description: A Magellanic penguin swims around the planet 12 times during its lifetime, according to scientists tracking penguin migration routes from a colony in Argentina.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 27
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, April 27th: Correspondents Dinner shooting suspect railed against Trump policies in writings; Suspect in custody after Saturday night shooting; U.S. strike on suspected drug boat; King Charles to visit United States.

video:video.video:title: Mayor Mamdani and Governor Hochul announce 5 free FIFA fan events across New York City
video:video.video:description: While the region is estimated to generate over 3.3 billion in economic activity throughout the tournament, Governor Hochul expressed the importance to ensure the people who live in New York “don't feel overlooked”.

video:video.video:title: Tariff refund portal woes add insult to injury for small business owners caught in Trump’s trade war
video:video.video:description: Beth Benike says technical problems have stymied efforts to collect $50,000 in tariff refunds she paid to get her baby mat products into the country.

video:video.video:title: The Netherlands is turning orange to celebrate King Willem-Alexander's birthday
video:video.video:description: Amsterdam's canals were packed with boats carrying orange-clad revellers on Monday, as the Netherlands celebrated King Willem Alexander's 59th birthday.

video:video.video:title: Virginia Supreme Court weighs legality of Democratic redistricting plan after voters approved it
video:video.video:description: Virginia's Supreme Court is considering whether a voter-approved redistricting amendment complied with the state's constitutional requirements, a case that carries high stakes for the balance of power in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Donald and Melania Trump call for ABC to fire Jimmy Kimmel after morbid joke about first lady
video:video.video:description: Both President Donald Trump and his wife, Melania, are calling on ABC to fire Jimmy Kimmel after the late-night comic joked last week that the first lady had “the glow of an expectant widow.”

video:video.video:title: Will George Clooney guest on 'The Pitt'?
video:video.video:description: George Clooney loves his "ER" co-stars - so will fans see him reunited with Noah Wyle on "The Pitt?"

video:video.video:title: Trumps greet King Charles III and Queen Camilla at the White House
video:video.video:description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrived at the White House Monday for a four-day U.S. visit celebrating America’s 250th anniversary.
video:video.video:tag: George III of the United Kingdom

video:video.video:title: ‘Dances With Wolves’ actor Nathan Chasing Horse sentenced to life in prison for sexual assault
video:video.video:description: Nathan Chasing Horse has been sentenced to life in prison for sexual assault. The “Dances With Wolves” actor was found guilty in January of 13 charges in a yearslong sexual abuse case.

video:video.video:title: AP investigates why many adopted kids end up in residential treatment centers
video:video.video:description: An Associated Press investigation finds a business known for tough-love boarding schools for rebellious teenagers has set its sights on a different demographic: adopted kids.

video:video.video:title: Tornadoes in northern Texas leave at least 2 dead and destroy multiple homes
video:video.video:description: National Weather Service teams confirmed that an EF-2 tornado with peak winds of 135 mph (217 kph) touched down in the Runaway Bay area.

video:video.video:title: Baby on board: Paramedics help passenger give birth just before Delta flight lands
video:video.video:description: Two paramedics helped deliver a baby on a Delta flight as it landed in Portland, Oregon. Ashley Blair gave birth to her daughter on Friday night.

video:video.video:title: US energy blockade leaves Cuban farmers struggling to feed a nation
video:video.video:description: In Cuba farmers are struggling to grow and transport food. (AP/ Danica Coto, Ariel Fernández and Milexsy Durán)

video:video.video:title: US energy blockade leaves Cuban farmers struggling to feed a nation
video:video.video:description: In Cuba farmers are struggling to grow and transport food. (AP/ Danica Coto, Ariel Fernández and Milexsy Durán)

video:video.video:title: Historic Route 66 diner partly collapses in New Mexico, no injuries reported
video:video.video:description: A portion of the east wall of Lindy’s Diner, a historic Route 66 diner in downtown Albuquerque, collapsed Monday afternoon, sending heavy dust across the sidewalk, according to the City of Albuquerque.

video:video.video:title: King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrive at British embassy to host garden party
video:video.video:description: Two and a half centuries after the American colonies declared independence from Britain under King George III, his descendant King Charles III arrived at the White House on Monday with trans-Atlantic ties under strain and security in the spotlight.
video:video.video:tag: George III of the United Kingdom

video:video.video:title: Tired and worried, seafarers have been stranded in the Persian Gulf for weeks
video:video.video:description: Stranded off Oman for more than a month, Reza Muhammad Saleh, an Indonesian chief officer aboard a Greek-owned cargo ship, said a drone exploded near the port shortly after they arrived March 3.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers recover last victims from Indonesia train wreck that killed 14 and injured dozens
video:video.video:description: Rescuers have finished removing victims from a damaged commuter train car, confirming the crash outside Indonesia’s capital killed 14 people. (AP Video: Ahmad Ibrahim)

video:video.video:title: Russia’s Putin meets with Iranian FM Araghchi in Saint Petersburg
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin met with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in Saint Petersburg on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Republicans amp up their push for White House ballroom
video:video.video:description: A group of Republican Senators led by Lindsey Graham are pushing legislation to provide federal funding for a ballroom on the site of the former East Wing of the White House.

video:video.video:title: Archaeologists reconstruct the face of a man fleeing the Pompeii eruption using AI
video:video.video:description: Archaeologists at the ancient Roman site of Pompeii have used artificial intelligence to digitally reconstruct the face of a victim of the AD 79 eruption of Mount Vesuvius.

video:video.video:title: Breaking the Ice: 'Heated Rivalry' fuels LGBTQ+ hockey growth
video:video.video:description: The San Francisco Earthquakes are experiencing a surge in interest and attendance, fueled in part by the hit show "Heated Rivalry," which has helped more LGBTQ+ players feel seen in a sometimes unwelcoming sport.

video:video.video:title: US soldier charged with using intel to win $400K on Maduro appears in court in NYC
video:video.video:description: A U.S. soldier is in court in New York, accused of using classified information about a mission targeting Nicolás Maduro in Venezuela to win money on the prediction market Polymarket.

video:video.video:title: White House welcome for King Charles III includes parade and flyover
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump and King Charles III greet each other warmly as the monarch begins a day of diplomacy that started with a White House ceremony.

video:video.video:title: First lady, Queen Camilla join students to see US-UK history through 21st century lens
video:video.video:description: First Lady Melania Trump hosted Queen Camilla at the White House for a look at U.S.-U.K. history through the lens of 21st century innovation.

video:video.video:title: Trump hails the 'cherished bond' between the US and UK as he welcomes royals to the White House
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump hailed the “cherished bond” between the United States and the United Kingdom Tuesday as he welcomed King Charles III and Queen Camilla for a state visit aimed at highlighting ties that hold firm and endure despite political strain.

video:video.video:title: Mississippi middle school students stop bus from crashing after driver blacks out
video:video.video:description: After their bus driver blacked out during an asthma attack, five Mississippi middle schoolers worked together to stop the bus, call for help and give their bus driver her medication.

video:video.video:title: Michael B. Jordan brings niece and nephew to 'Swapped' premiere
video:video.video:description: Michael B. Jordan shares a moment with his niece and nephew at the premiere of his Netflix animated film "Swapped."

video:video.video:title: Author and director of 'Heated Rivalry' explain the hit show's appeal
video:video.video:description: Author Rachel Reid and writer-director Jacob Tierney say they would enjoy "Heated Rivalry" even if they were not its creators.

video:video.video:title: Mel C: 'Before I was part of the Spice Girls, I discovered rave culture' | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Get your heart pumpin'. She'll make you “Sweat.” Such is the promise sung by the artist known as Melanie C, or Mel C and Sporty Spice of the game-changing ‘90s girl group Spice Girls, in the lead single from her ninth album of the same name.

video:video.video:title: Ex-NBA player Damon Jones is 1st to plead guilty in gambling sweep that led to over 30 arrests
video:video.video:description: Jones pleaded guilty to two counts of conspiracy to commit wire fraud for his role in what prosecutors say were sports betting and rigged poker game schemes.

video:video.video:title: US military boards commercial ship suspected of attempting to evade Iran blockade
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military on Tuesday said it boarded a commercial ship suspected of attempting to transit to Iran in violation of the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports.

video:video.video:title: Robot dogs with Musk and Zuckerberg heads roam around Berlin museum in Beeple's new exhibit
video:video.video:description: Robot dogs with hyper-realistic silicone heads modeled after famous figures like Elon Musk and Mark Zuckerberg are roaming a Berlin museum.

video:video.video:title: Ships wait in Strait of Hormuz with US and Iran locked in a standoff
video:video.video:description: Two regional officials say that Iran has offered to end its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for the U.S. lifting its blockade on the country and an end to the war.

video:video.video:title: Marathon record-breaker Sawe credits second place runner after smashing 2-hour barrier
video:video.video:description: Kenyan distance runner Sabastian Sawe pulled off a feat that was long considered unthinkable, winning the London Marathon on Sunday in a time of 1 hour, 59 minutes and 30 seconds.

video:video.video:title: Most sunscreens harm corals. Here's what you can do
video:video.video:description: About 25% of applied sunscreen washes off into the water, releasing thousands of tons of toxic chemicals annually in reef areas.

video:video.video:title: Students want “AI-proof” majors — but no one knows what they are
video:video.video:description: Students are searching for “AI-proof” majors as AI changes the job market — but no one knows what actually counts.

video:video.video:title: Richard Gadd turned down job offers to make 'Half Man'
video:video.video:description: Hollywood came calling after the success of Gadd's series "Baby Reindeer." (April 27)

video:video.video:title: Sawe makes history with first sub-2-hour marathon
video:video.video:description: Sabastian Sawe of Kenya makes history, becoming the first to run a record-eligible marathon in under two hours, winning the London Marathon in 1 hour, 59 minutes and 30 seconds.

video:video.video:title: AP Newsminute 27 P
video:video.video:description: Here are the top stories for Monday, April 27; Accused assailant at D.C. media dinner charged with attempted assassination; Iran's foreign minister meets with Putin; King Charles arrives in D.C.; Rain aids fight against Georgia wildfires.

video:video.video:title: White House blasts correspondents' dinner shooting conspiracies as 'crazy nonsense'
video:video.video:description: Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt slammed conspiracy theories suggesting the White House Correspondents Dinner shooting Saturday night was staged, calling it "crazy nonsense" and urging "maximum transparency."

video:video.video:title: Spice Girl Melanie C wants to bring joy to the club with latest album
video:video.video:description: Melanie C talks 'Sweat,' a new album that channels her pre-Spice Girls rave roots. (April 27)

video:video.video:title: Sumatran orangutan uses canopy bridge to cross road in Indonesia
video:video.video:description: A Sumatran orangutan has been filmed for the first time using a human-made canopy bridge to cross a public road on the Indonesian island of Sumatra, conservationists said Monday.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds protest in Colombia for a transition away from fossil fuels
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of protesters took to the streets of the Colombian city of Santa Marta on Monday to demand a transition away from fossil fuels. (AP Video by Samuel Sotomayor.

video:video.video:title: Extreme heat could impact the World Cup games. Here's what cities are doing to prepare
video:video.video:description: The 2022 World Cup tournament in Qatar was moved from summer to winter because of the threat of extreme heat.

video:video.video:title: Trump pursues new import taxes to replace the tariffs the Supreme Court rejected
video:video.video:description: When the Supreme Court killed his favorite tariffs in February, President Donald Trump rolled out temporary import taxes to replace them.

video:video.video:title: New supersized telescope in Atacama desert could offer view of Earth-like planets
video:video.video:description: With more than 300 clear nights a year, Chile's Atacama has become home to the world’s largest ground-based astronomical projects.

video:video.video:title: Michael B. Jordan on his 'Academy Award winner' title
video:video.video:description: Here's how Academy Award winner Michael B. Jordan feels about being called that. His new film is "Swapped."

video:video.video:title: Richard Gadd turned down job offers to make 'Half Man'
video:video.video:description: After creating and starring in the Netflix hit "Baby Reindeer," Richard Gadd says he turned down "surreal" opportunities to act alongside "amazing people." Here's why.

video:video.video:title: King Charles III says US-UK alliance ‘cannot rest on past achievements’
video:video.video:description: King Charles III delivered a speech to a joint meeting of Congress that highlighted the bonds between the two countries.

video:video.video:title: United Arab Emirates says it will leave OPEC in a blow to the oil cartel
video:video.video:description: The decision by the United Arab Emirates to leave the OPEC oil cartel shook up the 65-year-old alliance that produces some 40% of the world’s crude oil and exerts major influence over the price of energy around the globe.

video:video.video:title: Man pleads guilty to plotting attack on a Taylor Swift concert in Vienna
video:video.video:description: A man accused of plotting to attack one of Taylor Swift’s concerts in Vienna nearly two years ago has pleaded guilty as his trial began on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Ex-FBI Director Comey indicted in probe over online post officials call a Trump threat
video:video.video:description: Former FBI Director James Comey has been indicted in a probe over a social media photo of seashells arranged on a beach that officials claim constituted a threat against President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: AI reconstructs the potential face of a man killed in Pompeii eruption
video:video.video:description: Researchers in Pompeii used AI to reconstruct the potential face of a man killed in the AD 79 Mount Vesuvius eruption, offering a new way to understand the disaster.

video:video.video:title: Billie Eilish arrives at concert movie premiere in London
video:video.video:description: Billie Eilish is joined by her co-director James Cameron at the "Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour" concert movie premiere in London. The film hits theaters May 8.

video:video.video:title: Julianna Margulies says bond with George Clooney and Noah Wyle ‘are for life’
video:video.video:description: Julianna Margulies describes her bond with "ER" castmates George Clooney and Noah Wyle.

video:video.video:title: South Korea and New Zealand conduct joint amphibious drill
video:video.video:description: Troops from South Korea and New Zealand took part in an amphibious exercise off the coats of Pohang on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Julianna Margulies says bond with E.R. castmates Clooney, Wyle: ‘are for life’
video:video.video:description: Julianna Margulies reflects on the bond with ER castmates that included George Clooney, Noah Wyle, and Anthony Edwards. (April 27)

video:video.video:title: Starmer’s former chief of staff admits ‘serious mistake’ in recommending Mandelson appointment
video:video.video:description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer's former chief of staff said Tuesday that he made a “serious mistake” by recommending Peter Mandelson be made U.K. ambassador to the United States, but denied interfering with the appointment process.

video:video.video:title: King Charles highlights US-UK bond in speech to Congress
video:video.video:description: King Charles III is only the second British monarch to address a joint session of Congress, after his mother, Queen Elizabeth II, delivered a similar speech in 1991.

video:video.video:title: King Charles III acknowledges 'times of great uncertainty' during congressional speech
video:video.video:description: King Charles III acknowledges “times of great uncertainty” as he expresses gratitude to the American people and marks the 250th anniversary of independence from Britain in a speech to the U.S.

video:video.video:title: As raw milk push grows, farmers show the pasteurization process
video:video.video:description: Proponents of raw milk are pushing to make the unpasteurized product more widely available and easy to obtain.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 28
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, April 28th: King Charles III addresses Congress; Comey indicted in probe over online post; soldier charged with using intel to win $400K on Maduro raid appears in court and robot dogs with Musk, Bezos, Zuckerberg and Warhol heads.

video:video.video:title: King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrive at White House for state dinner
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump and first lady Melania Trump greet King Charles III and Queen Camilla at the South Portico of the White House as they arrive for a state dinner.

video:video.video:title: Colombian president Gustavo Petro warns of existential dangers posed by fossil fuels
video:video.video:description: Addressing a plenary at a high-level climate conference, Colombian president Gustavo Petro addresses existential dangers posed by fossil fuels.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Russian oil refinery on fire again after Ukrainian drone strike
video:video.video:description: In Russia’s southern Krasnodar region, fires were reported at an oil refinery at the Black Sea port of Tuapse again, local officials said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Trump celebrates cultural ties with the UK during state dinner toast with King Charles III
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump hosted the King Charles III and Queen Camilla for a jovial state dinner Tuesday in the East Room of the White House.

video:video.video:title: Young Palestinian artists in Gaza exhibit their impressions of war as a fragile ceasefire holds
video:video.video:description: Palestinian artists participated in an Art Festival in Central Gaza's Bureij on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Skiers hit the slopes at dawn as late snow extends season
video:video.video:description: Skiers in Bosnia are hitting the slopes before dawn for a race down Mount Bjelasnica. A late-season snowfall has helped extend the ski season this year and created the perfect conditions for the unusual event.

video:video.video:title: Janet Mills has the bona fides. Is that enough to win over Maine's primary voters?
video:video.video:description: Maine Gov. Janet Mills has decades of experience in office and the backing of the party establishment as she seeks the Democratic nomination for U.S. Senate.

video:video.video:title: New Orleans' Jazz Fest honors Jamaican heritage with music, food and craftsmanship
video:video.video:description: Thousands flock to New Orleans' Jazz and Heritage festival each year where this year's Cultural Exchange Pavilion is honoring Jamaica, including communities impacted by Hurricane Melissa.

video:video.video:title: King Charles highlights US-UK bond during state dinner with Trump
video:video.video:description: King Charles III on Tuesday praised the ties between the U.S. and the UK, calling the relationship "an unbreakable bond of history and heritage," during a jovial state dinner at the White House.

video:video.video:title: With large-scale reconstruction in Gaza yet to begin, people start building their own homes
video:video.video:description: After months of displacement, 34-year-old Nassim al-Farra is building a home for his family from the remains of a destroyed building in southern Gaza.

video:video.video:title: Rare earth mining is poisoning Mekong River tributaries, threatening 'the world's kitchen'
video:video.video:description: Toxic runoff from rare earth mines in Myanmar is contaminating rivers that flow into Thailand, threatening the Mekong River and its basin's fisheries and farmland.

video:video.video:title: King Charles presents Trump with bell from WWII UK submarine 'HMS Trump' during state dinner toast
video:video.video:description: King Charles III presented President Donald Trump with the original conning tower bell from the WWII-era submarine HMS Trump, during a state dinner in Washington on Tuesday night.

video:video.video:title: Hungry locals rush for last orders at much loved soul food cafe being closed by Hong Kong redevelopment
video:video.video:description: When the bustling Sun Lung Shing Teahouse shuts up shop in an old rundown alleyway in Hong Kong, hungry locals will be mourning the loss of a bygone era.

video:video.video:title: Sudan war's hidden danger: unexploded weapons have injured hundreds
video:video.video:description: As residents return to Sudan's capital Khartoum after months of fighting, unexploded ordnance and landmines left behind by the war are creating a growing danger across the capital.

video:video.video:title: At least 2 injured as storms tear through Texas, damaging homes
video:video.video:description: Thunderstorms tore through parts of Texas on Tuesday, sending at least two people to the hospital as powerful winds ripped roofs off homes, flattened buildings and tossed debris through the air.
